volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 3.4.620 - Section 4.1.686)

sections.label: Section 3.4.620
sections.translation: Tell me , who bought her ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.620b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do not know .

sections.label: Section 3.4.620c
sections.translation: Well , is this a good friend giving one his aid ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.621b
sections.translation: What would you have me do ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.621c
sections.translation: The same that you see me doing , die with grief . But did you make enquiry , what was the appearance of the person that had bought her ? Perhaps the damsel might have been traced out by that means .

sections.label: Section 3.4.624
sections.translation: Ah ! wretch that I am —

sections.label: Section 3.4.624b
sections.original: Flere omitte , istuc quod nunc agis .
sections.translation: Do cease lamenting ; attend to that which you are now about .

sections.label: Section 3.4.625
sections.translation: What have I done ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.625b
sections.original: Perdidisti me et fidem mecum tuam .
sections.translation: Proved the destruction of myself , and with myself of your own word .

sections.label: Section 3.4.626
sections.original: Di sciunt culpam meam istanc non esse ullam .
sections.translation: The Gods know that that is not any fault of mine .

sections.label: Section 3.4.626b
sections.translation: A fine thing , indeed ! You mention the Gods , who are absent , as witnesses ; how am I to believe you in that ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.628
sections.original: Quia tibi in manu est quod credas , ego quod dicam , id mi in manust .
sections.translation: Why , it rests with your own self what to believe ; with myself , what to say , that rests with me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.629
sections.original: De istac ré argutus es , ut par pari respondeas ,
sections.translation: On that point you are ready , so as to give answer like for like ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.630
sections.original: ad mandata claudus caecus mutus mancus debilis .
sections.translation: but as to what you are requested , you are lame , blind , dumb , defective , and weak . You promised that you would trick my father ; I myself supposed that I was entrusting the matter to a skilful person , and I entrusted it to an utter stone .

sections.label: Section 3.4.633
sections.translation: What could I do ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.633b
sections.original: Quid tu faceres ? men rogas ? requireres ,
sections.translation: What could you do , do you ask me ? You should have enquired , and asked who he was or whence he was , of what lineage ;

sections.label: Section 3.4.635
sections.translation: whether he was a citizen or a foreigner —

sections.label: Section 3.4.635b
sections.translation: They said that he was a citizen of Attica .

sections.label: Section 3.4.636
sections.original: Vbi habitaret invenires saltem , si nomen nequis .
sections.translation: At least , you should have found out where he lives , if you couldn ’ t the name .

sections.label: Section 3.4.637
sections.translation: No person was able to say he knew .

sections.label: Section 3.4.637b
sections.translation: But at least you should have enquired what was the appearance of the man .

sections.label: Section 3.4.638
sections.translation: I did do so .

sections.label: Section 3.4.638b
sections.translation: Of what figure , then , did they say he was ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.638c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you : grey-headed , bandy-legged , pot-bellied , wide-mouthed , of stunted figure ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.640
sections.original: subnigris oculis , oblongis malis , pansam aliquantulum .
sections.translation: with darkish eyes , lank jaws , splay-footed rather .

sections.label: Section 3.4.641
sections.original: Non hominem mihi , sed thensaurum nescio quem memoras mali .
sections.translation: You are mentioning to me not a human being , but a whole storehouse , I don ’ t know what , of deformities . Is there anything else that you can tell about him ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.642b
sections.translation: It is just as much as I know .

sections.label: Section 3.4.643
sections.original: Edepol ne ille oblongis malis mihi dedit magnum malum .
sections.translation: I ’ troth , for sure , with his lank jaws he has caused my jaw to drop [*] . I cannot endure it ; I ’ m determined that I ’ ll go hence in exile .

sections.label: Section 3.4.645
sections.original: sed quam capiam civitatem , cogito , potissimum :
sections.translation: But what state in especial to repair to , I ’ m in doubt ; Megara , Eretria , Corinth , Chalcis , Crete , Cyprus , Sicyon , Cnidos , Zacynthus , Lesbos , or Bœotia .

sections.label: Section 3.4.648
sections.translation: Why are you adopting that design ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.648b
sections.translation: Why , because love is tormenting me .

sections.label: Section 3.4.649
sections.original: Quid tu ais ? quid cum illuc , quo nunc ire paritas , veneris ,
sections.translation: What say you as to this ? Suppose , if when you have arrived there , whither you are now intending to go ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.650
sections.original: sí ibi amare forte occipias atque item eius sit inopia ,
sections.translation: you begin there to fall desperately in love , and there , too , you fail of success , then you ’ ll be taking flight from there as well , and after that , again , from another place , if the same shall happen , what bounds , pray , will be set to your exile , what limits to your flight ? What country or home can possibly be certain for you ? Tell me that . Say now , if you leave this city , do you fancy that you ’ ll leave your love here behind ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.655
sections.original: si id fore ita sat animo acceptum est , certum id , pro certo si habes ,
sections.translation: If it is so fully taken as certain in your mind that so it will be , if you hold that as a point resolved upon , how much better is it for you to go away somewhere in the country , to be there , to live there , until the time when desire for her and passion have set you at liberty ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.658
sections.translation: Have you now said your say ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.658b
sections.translation: I have said it .

sections.label: Section 3.4.658c
sections.translation: You have said it to no purpose ; this is my full determination . I ’ ll be off home , to pay my duty to my father and my mother ; after that ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.660
sections.original: clam patrem patria hac effugiam , aut aliquid capiam consili .—
sections.translation: unknown to my father , I ’ ll fly from this country , or adopt some other plan .

sections.label: Section 3.4.661
sections.original: Vt corripuit se repente atque abiit . heu misero mihi ,
sections.translation: How suddenly he has taken himself off and gone away . Ah ! wretch that I am ! if he goes away , all will say that it has happened through my remissness . I ’ m determined at once to order as many criers as possible to be hired to search for her to find her ; after that , I ’ ll go to the Praetor forthwith ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.665
sections.original: ibo , orabo , ut conquaestores det mi in vicis omnibus ;
sections.translation: and beg him to give me search-warrant officers in all the quarters of the city ; for I find that nothing else whatever is now left for me to do .

sections.label: Section 4.1.667
sections.original: Quoniam a viro ad me rus advenit nuntius ,
sections.translation: Since a messenger came to me in the country from my husband , that he couldn ’ t come into the country , I made up my mind , and came back to follow after him who fled from me .

sections.label: Section 4.1.670
sections.original: sed anum non video consequi nostram Syram .
sections.translation: But ( looking round ) I don ’ t see our old woman Syra following . Aye , look , there she comes at last . ( Enter SYRA with a bundle of green sprigs .) Why don ’ t you go quicker ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.672
sections.original: nequeo mecastor , tantum hoc onerist quod fero .
sections.translation: By my troth , I cannot ; so great is this burden that I ’ m carrying .

sections.label: Section 4.1.673
sections.translation: What burden ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.673b
sections.translation: Fourscore years and four , and to that are added servitude , sweat , and thirst ;

sections.label: Section 4.1.675
sections.translation: these things as well which I am carrying weigh me down .

sections.label: Section 4.1.675b
sections.translation: Give me something , Syra , with which to decorate this altar of our neighbour [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.1.677
sections.original: da sane hanc virgam lauri . abi tu intro .
sections.translation: Present this sprig of laurel , then .

sections.label: Section 4.1.677b
sections.translation: I ’ m going .

sections.label: Section 4.1.678
sections.original: Apollo , quaeso te , ut des pacem propitius ,
sections.translation: Apollo , I pray thee that thou wilt propitiously grant peace , safety , and health , unto our household ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.680
sections.original: meoque ut parcas gnato pace propitius .
sections.translation: and that in thy propitiousness thou wilt show favour to my son .

sections.label: Section 4.1.681
sections.original: Disperii , perii misera , vae miserae mihi .
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone ! Wretch that I am , I ’ m ruined ! Ah ! wretched me !

sections.label: Section 4.1.682
sections.original: Satin tu sana es , obsecro ? quid eiulas ?
sections.translation: Prithee , are you quite in your senses ? What are you howling for ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.683
sections.translation: Dorippa , my dear Dorippa !

sections.label: Section 4.1.683b
sections.translation: Prithee , why are you crying out ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.684
sections.original: Nescio quaest mulier intus hic in aedibus .
sections.translation: Some woman , I know not who , is here in-doors in the house .

sections.label: Section 4.1.685
sections.translation: What ? A woman ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.685b
sections.translation: A harlot woman .

sections.label: Section 4.1.685c
sections.translation: Is it so , really ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.686
sections.original: Nimium scis sapere , ruri quae non manseris .
sections.translation: In serious truth . You know how to act very prudently , in not remaining in the country . A fool even could have found it out that she was the mistress of your very pretty husband .
